quarters.key_risks: You know, if you think about the backlog, I think right now roughly 80% is, you know, products and software and the rest, you know, I'd think about is as services. | I think you just said 80% of the $7 billion is products and software. | If I just apply that 80% to the $5 billion, that's implying $1.6 billion in product and software growth, which, you know, loose math and loose assumptions there.
quarters.key_improvements: Positive guidance read supported by: The price increases that we talked about at the end of last year, those really haven't started to fully kick in until the second half of... | Management sounded credible and reasonably specific; answers were mostly direct: Given all these opportunities, we're confident our momentum will extend beyond 2026. | We had a very strong performance, and we're quite happy with the 44.7% that we printed this morning.
quarters.key_evidence: The price increases that we talked about at the end of last year, those really haven't started to fully kick in until the second half of the year. | We have visibility to, you know, a fair amount of it, but not all of it. | Because we had such a, you know, a big backlog at the time, most of that was gonna be seen in the second half. | We saw customers requiring increased capacity, both in hyperscalers and in service providers that increase their infill rates, and so we got quite a bit of tailwind from that.
